title: Sables Dominate Rugby Africa Cup Qualifier
excerpt: Zimbabwe's national team put on a clinical display to secure their place in the Rugby Africa Cup finals.

title: Sables Named for Rugby Africa Cup Qualifier
excerpt: Head coach Brendan Dawson has announced a 30-man squad for the upcoming Rugby Africa Cup qualifier against Kenya in Nairobi.
content: Head coach Brendan Dawson has announced a 30-man squad for the upcoming Rugby Africa Cup qualifier against Kenya in Nairobi. The squad features eight uncapped players as Zimbabwe looks to blood new talent ahead of the Rugby World Cup 2027 qualifying campaign. 'We have a strong mix of experience and youth,' said Dawson at the squad announcement in Harare. 'The boys are ready and we know what is at stake.'

title: Zimbabwe Defeats Uganda 31-18 in Thrilling Rugby Africa Cup Opener
excerpt: The Sables delivered a dominant performance at Harare Sports Club, running in four tries to defeat Uganda and claim their first win of the tournament.
content: The Sables delivered a dominant performance at Harare Sports Club, running in four tries to defeat Uganda and claim their first win of the Rugby Africa Cup. Fly-half Brendan Munyai was outstanding, contributing 16 points from boot and hand. The victory puts Zimbabwe top of Pool B with a crucial quarter-final spot within reach.

title: ZRU Launches Grassroots Schools Rugby Programme
excerpt: The Zimbabwe Rugby Union has officially launched a nationwide schools development programme targeting over 200 secondary schools across all provinces.
content: The Zimbabwe Rugby Union has officially launched a nationwide schools development programme targeting over 200 secondary schools across all provinces. The initiative, backed by World Rugby's Get Into Rugby programme, aims to introduce the sport to an estimated 50,000 pupils in its first year. ZRU CEO Brenda Mudzimuirema described it as 'the most ambitious grassroots initiative in our history.'

title: Old Mutual Signs Three-Year Sponsorship Deal with ZRU
excerpt: Financial services giant Old Mutual has signed a landmark three-year sponsorship agreement with the Zimbabwe Rugby Union, providing critical funding for the national programme.
content: Financial services giant Old Mutual has signed a landmark three-year sponsorship agreement with the Zimbabwe Rugby Union. The deal, reported to be worth $1.2 million over three years, will fund the national programme including player contracts, coaching staff, and touring expenses. ZRU President Aaron Jani called it 'a game-changing moment for Zimbabwean rugby.'
